original_abstract: This research examines how the intersection of discipline and religious values shapes student resilience and responsibility at SMP Nidaul Quran in Kabupaten Sarolangun. Using a qualitative case study design, data were collected through in-depth interviews, field observations, and an analysis of institutional documents. The findings reveal that discipline serves as a foundational pillar for character development, moving beyond mere rule enforcement to facilitate the internalisation of core virtues. By harmonising behavioral discipline with Islamic spiritual practices, the school successfully fosters students who are both resilient in the face of adversity and accountable for their actions. Key to this process is the role of teachers as moral exemplars and the proactive involvement of parents, creating a robust support system. This study suggests that integrating religious values does more than bolster academic competence; it anchors students with strong moral integrity. The holistic model employed by SMP Nidaul Quran—linking educators, families, and faith—offers a scalable framework for other Islamic educational institutions seeking to cultivate the "hearts and souls" of students, ensuring they are prepared for the complexities of modern life.
humanized_abstract: Titlе: Shaping Student Resilience and Accountability: The Roles of Discipline and Islamic Values at a Pondok School in Indonesia Abstract : Student discipline is often associated with the enforcement of school rules and hence the maintenance of order and peace at school. It is, however, less often related to the instruction and education of students’ characters at the same time enhancing their survival skills and hence increasing their level of resilience. But what happens when such disciplines are incorporated within the ambit of religion and made more relevant within the context of an Islamic school, specifically SMP Nidaul Quran in Kabupaten Sarolangun. The study that was carried out based on qualitative case study research revealed that discipline and religion have a lasting influence on student discipline and thereby impacting positively on their characters and ability to withstand pressure while being held responsible at all times. The teachers carried out their duties efficiently by leading the students along the right path towards greater discipline in line with the goal of the school management while parents were also supportive and encouraged the pupils to adhere strictly to the rules set out by the teachers and also the school management. Thus, a strong bond of discipline and religion increases student’s accountability for their deeds and at the same time increases the resilience of the students. This is further supported by the fact that the school used the whole school approach which involved educators, families and religion hence enhancing students’ discipline within the school premises as well as promoting students’ all round development as successful students in this 21 century. Hence, other Pondok Schools that are yearning to mould the students into individuals of integrity whom they can send forward to face greater challenges in this 21 century as countries develop, should as well adopt the whole school approach of instructing students to strengthen their hearts and souls.
